Title: Natural variation in LONELY GUY-Like 1 regulates rice grain weight under warmer night conditions

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Cytokinin activation; indirect regulation of thiamin biosynthesis pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Conversion of inactive cytokinin nucleotides/ribosides to active cytokinin free-base forms
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Putatively converts inactive cytokinin nucleotides to active free-base cytokinins such as trans-zeatin and isopentenyl adenine
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Inactive cytokinin nucleotides/ribosides including trans-zeatin riboside-related precursors
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Active cytokinin forms including trans-zeatin (tZ) and likely iP
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Indirectly regulates grain-weight-associated metabolism and thiamin pathway through cytokinin activation
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Kitaake; Rice Diversity Panel 1 accessions; tropical japonica subset in field tents
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly active during reproductive development; high expression in developing grains at 2 days after fertilization
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher transcript abundance in LGA than HGA allelic group; highly expressed in developing grains at 2 DAF
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: trans-zeatin; thiamin-related pathway output linked to grain weight
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cytokinin promotes endosperm cell division and sink activity; thiamin supports carbon metabolism and oxidative stress responses
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Lower LOGL1 expression or loss of function increases single grain weight under HNT; overexpression increases HNT sensitivity and reduces grain number, fertility, and total grain weight per plant under HNT
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; allelic expression analysis; RT-qPCR in developing grains; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; constitutive overexpression under maize ubiquitin promoter; field and greenhouse HNT phenotyping; RNA-seq; cytokinin metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High nighttime temperature during grain development/post-flowering (30/28°C greenhouse; ~4°C above ambient at night in field tents)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: GWAS using 411,066 SNPs; RNA-seq BioProject PRJNA855943; public transcriptome datasets GSE6893 and GSE98455
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HNT-specific GWAS peak SGW1 mapped to the first intron of LOGL1. 2. Accessions carrying the favorable HGA allele had lower LOGL1 transcript abundance and higher single grain weight under HNT. 3. CRISPR logl1 mutants showed higher grain weight than WT under HNT, whereas LOGL1 overexpressers were more HNT-sensitive. 4. LOGL1 misregulation altered cytokinin activation status, circadian/thiamin pathway gene expression, and HNT-induced ROS-associated transcriptional responses. 5. LOGL1 also affected tiller number, grain number, fertility, and total grain yield components.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression increased SGW but reduced tiller number, grain number, fertility, and total grain weight per plant, especially under HNT; constitutive misexpression may cause unfavorable source-sink tradeoffs
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Select low-expression favorable SGW1/LOGL1 alleles for warmer-night environments; stack favorable SGW1 allele with qSGW6 favorable alleles; genome editing or downregulation is preferable to constitutive overexpression because OE increases HNT sensitivity and yield penalties
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Maize ubiquitin 1 promoter, constitutive (for overexpression lines)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although OE lines had higher SGW than WT, this was interpreted as a compensatory effect caused by fewer grains and reduced sink number rather than improved HNT tolerance; LOGL1 effect is strongest under HNT and likely mediated indirectly through cytokinin-circadian-thiamin/ROS networks
